rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible or properly declared in the same annex scope.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by correcting invalid helper declarations or removing problematic clauses.
generic_rules: When validator reports unresolved references, trace the first invalid identifier on the same line and correct the root cause.
If a clause references an invalid or missing symbol, remove the clause rather than adding new architecture elements.
AGREE expressions must have consistent types; fix type mismatches by aligning operand types or correcting declarations.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses must be syntactically valid and refer to properly declared identifiers or visible AADL features.
Ensure all expressions in AGREE contracts have matching types to prevent '<error>' type mismatches.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new architecture elements to fix AGREE resolution errors; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by introducing new component ports or features in the wrong scope.
irrelevant_topics: Component type vs implementation scope placement rules
